items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: Two months ago I gave my first ever live programming talk at KotlinConf. Part of my talk-writing process is lots of procrastination and distraction. This year that materialized in writing a tool to help my code stay on track during the presentation. https:// jakewharton.com/live-coding-wi th-dir-stepper/

items.summary: Short video from Usagi Electric on a classic teletype that sends encoded data serially over RS232 which is the foundation for today's /dev/tty that drives modern terminal emulation. https://www. youtube.com/watch?v=31JBOlrzPXs

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: It's interesting seeing Zig folk laud their assert function in contrast to C's assert macro, when it seems like in Kotlin the assert function behavior is criticized in comparison to Java's assert language keyword. I definitely want the macro/Java-like behavior but with a lambda syntax to make the effects (or lack thereof) more obvious. https:// cryptocode.g…

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: I don't use the Compose BOM, and I don't think you should either. The advantages it claims to provide are less than it actually does. Let's defuse the Compose BOM. https:// jakewharton.com/defuse-the-com pose-bom/

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: My chat with @ codewiththeitalians from yesterday is now available to watch on YouTube. We talk about why some people should be using the AndroidX betas, whether to adopt them directly or do shadow jobs, and finish with my setup for testing prerelease versions of open source libraries. And my dogs snore through the whole episode! https://www. youtube.com/wa…
